text.title: Design-to-Dev Handoff Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Design-to-Dev Handoff. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every handoff ships four artefacts: filled handoff-spec, ≤5-min walkthrough video, co-written acceptance criteria, scheduled 15-min handoff sync; partial packages are rejected.
rule.rationale: Each artefact closes a distinct gap: spec for ambiguity, video for nuance, AC for done-definition, sync for residual Q&A.

rule.statement: Acceptance criteria are co-written by designer + eng BEFORE code starts; designer-only AC is rejected.
rule.rationale: Designer-only AC misses eng constraints; co-written AC catches conflicts before code.

rule.statement: Walkthrough video explicitly names at least 3 gotchas (edge cases, expected behaviour, prior-version differences); pure prose tours are rejected.
rule.rationale: Generic walkthroughs cover what eng already infers; named gotchas surface what they would miss.

rule.statement: Handoff sync is either held within 7 days OR explicitly converted to async (Loom + threaded Qs) with engineering sign-off; silent drops are rejected.
rule.rationale: Silent drop produces re-litigation; recorded decision lets the package close.

rule.statement: Eng owner is a named handle; 'eng team' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Plural owner produces orphan handoffs; named owner gets pinged when blocked.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-design-to-dev-handoff.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
